name: HIKEN SUZUME KENJUTSU: KUROYOKU HISHOU (SECRET SPARROW SWORD ART: BLACK WING FLIGHT)

description.value:
Type: Taijutsu (Strike)

Rank: 4

Chakra Cost:

Learn DC: 16 | Successes: 2

Perform: 7 | Perform DC: 16

Components: C, F, M Mas

This technique is the first in the repertoire to divert an enemy's power away. While in a Hiken Suzume stance the user may parry just when an opponent prepares to strike a heavy blow, greatly reducing the damage to him. As an immediate action, the user may interrupt a single melee attack targeting him. To do so the user must be aware of the attack, else this technique may not be used. Avoiding an attack requires the user must make a Taijutsu check opposed by the enemy's Perform Check, with -5 penalty against non-Taijutsu techniques and critical hits. Success means that the technique's effect is reduced as if the user had made a successful save and the attacker may not apply bonus damage, such as that gained from Sneak Attack or a high Strength modifier. If the technique does not allow a saving throw, the user instead takes half damage.

This technique may only be performed before knowing if the attack was a success, and a failure to perform this technique results in the attack hitting automatically, though the user may still attempt a saving throw if allowed one.

Requires: Fuku Ejiki (3)

Material Focus: Bladed weapon.

Mastery: Each step of mastery allows this technique to be used an additional time per day up to a maximum of 5/day.

The first step of mastery allows the user to negate an attack up to his ECL, or 5, whichever is lower.

The second step of mastery allows the user to negate an attack up to his ECL, or 10, whichever is lower.

The third step of mastery allows the user to negate an attack up to his ECL, or 15, whichever is lower.

The fourth step of mastery allows the user to negate an attack up to his ECL, or 20, whichever is lower.

The fifth step of mastery allows the user to negate an attack up to his ECL, or 25, whichever is lower and to use this technique even when out of a Hiken Dachi stance.
